Danielle, welcome aboard. Before August, I'd like the academic team to know who they're getting. So, in your own words: what do you do?
I lead People and Culture at Maple Bear Brazil. My role involves the employee journey, every stage, from the first interview to the last day, and the people strategy behind it.
And how long have you been doing that?
I've been leading the culture transformation since 2023, and I've worked in People and Culture for eight years. I'm a psychologist by training.
I should be honest with you. Our people here are academics. Most of them hold doctorates. They'll ask you for evidence, not enthusiasm.
Good. I'm not arriving with a Brazilian model to install. What I bring is a framework, and the first thing I want to do is carry out a cultural assessment with each of them.
I lead People and Culture at Maple Bear, a franchisor in bilingual education.
My role involves the employee journey and the people strategy behind it.
At the moment, we're rolling out the framework across every region.

Danielle, this is Nathan Boyd, Vice President of Academic Operations at Maple Bear Canada. Welcome aboard. I wanted to leave you a proper introduction before we meet in August. Our academic team here is small but very senior. Most of them hold doctorates in education, and several of them have been with the franchisor since the very first Canadian school opened. They are curious about the work you have been leading in Brazil, and they have already asked me for the employee journey framework you built there. Now, a word of advice. Our senior leadership will not expect you to arrive with all the answers. They will expect you to ask very good questions. So take a stance, but leave room for their input. I will set up thirty minute one on one sessions with each of the leaders in your first two weeks. Talk soon.

Good morning, everyone, and thank you for joining this global people update. I want to open with the one number that matters. Ninety one percent of our people say they understand how their work connects to our strategy. Two years ago that number was fifty four percent. That is what alignment looks like when it is done properly. This year we are rolling out the new employee journey across every region, and headquarters will not do it for you. Each market owns its own culture. My team owns the framework. We are making headway on the hybrid model as well, although I will be honest with you, we are still learning. And a final word to our senior leadership. Culture is not a project that finishes. It is the way we behave when nobody is measuring us.
